seo.title: Pork Ribs that Stick and Shine - Marinda Kook

html:
For me a shiny sticky pork rib is one of the tastiest foods to eat. It's shiny, it's sticky and has such a light sweetness to it. I cooked it in the oven, but you can also braai it over the fire – just cook the potatoes and sweet potatoes separately. I kept the skins on the potatoes and sweet potatoes, just washed them well.

Ingredients

  • 1 kg pork ribs
  • 3 potatoes cut into thick slices
  • 2 onions cut into thick slices
  • 2 sweet potatoes cut into thick slices
  • 500 ml apple juice
  • rub of your choice, I used a Mexican rub
  • 250 ml tomato sauce
  • 125 ml brown sugar
  • 125 ml soy sauce
  • 125 ml balsamic vinegar

Method

  1. Pack the slices of potatoes, onions and sweet potatoes at the bottom of an oven dish
  2. Rub the pork ribs with the Mexican rub and place them on the vegetables in the oven dish.
  3. Pour the apple juice into the dish and cover the dish. (not over the pork, otherwise you'll rinse off all the rub) Bake it covered for 30 minutes at 180 C.
  4. Mix the tomato sauce, brown sugar, soy sauce and balsamic vinegar together. Pour half over the ribs and bake uncovered for 30 minutes
  5. After 30 minutes turn the ribs over and pour the other half of the sauce over. Bake again for 30 minutes.
  6. It's so tasty that you'll find it hard to share.
